skill.name: ckm:design
skill.description: Comprehensive design toolkit for brand identity, logos, social media graphics, banners, icons, presentations, and corporate identity programs. Uses Gemini AI for generation and produces SVG, HTML, and PNG output.
skill.summary: AI-powered design toolkit for creating logos, brand identities, social media images, icons, banners, and presentations.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: All 1099 static findings are false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings reference algorithm names in CSV data files containing design metadata, not actual cryptographic operations. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are CLI examples in Markdown code blocks. The skill legitimately requires env_access (Gemini API key), network calls (Gemini API), filesystem writes (design output), and external commands (image processing), all documented and expected for an AI design generation tool. No malicious intent or credential exfiltration detected. Published with medium risk warning due to elevated capabilities.

critical_findings.title: Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Combined
critical_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged DANGEROUS COMBINATION of code execution, network, and credential access across multiple files. DISMISSED: The combination is the legitimate function of an AI-powered design generation tool. API keys (GEMINI_API_KEY) are read from environment variables, network calls go to the Google Gemini API for AI generation, code execution runs design generation scripts, and filesystem writes produce output files. All capabilities are documented and expected.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Access for API Keys
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts read GEMINI_API_KEY from environment variables at runtime. FALSE POSITIVE: This is standard secure credential handling. Keys are not hardcoded. The scripts use os.environ.get() which is the recommended pattern for API key management.

dangerous_patterns.title: .env File Loading from Home Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: Custom load_env() function reads .env files from ~/.claude/ directory. FALSE POSITIVE: Standard configuration pattern for Claude Code skills. Only sets variables not already present in os.environ (prevents override). Reads from conventional skill configuration paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution in Python Scripts
dangerous_patterns.description: Python scripts use subprocess calls (backtick/run) for image processing. FALSE POSITIVE: Commands use hardcoded image processing tools (ImageMagick, etc.) with controlled arguments, not user-injected command strings. This is expected for design generation output.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm References in CSV Data
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer found algorithm names (RSA, AES, MD5, SHA) in CSV data files. FALSE POSITIVE: These are design metadata entries in CSV datasets (CIP deliverables, logo colors, icon styles), not executable cryptographic code. The data files contain design-related text that happens to include algorithm names.

dangerous_patterns.title: C2 Keywords in Industry/Style CSV Data
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer found C2 command-and-control keywords in CSV data files. FALSE POSITIVE: These are legitimate industry names and design style names in CSV datasets (e.g., industry names like 'command', style terms). No actual C2 infrastructure or communication is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Ruby Shell Backtick Execution in Reference Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer detected backtick shell execution patterns in Markdown reference files. FALSE POSITIVE: These are CLI examples in Markdown code blocks showing users how to run the design scripts. They are documentation, not executable code.

content.user_title: Design logos, brand identities, and social media visuals with AI
content.value_statement: Creating professional designs usually requires hiring a designer or learning complex software. This skill generates logos, brand identity documents, social media images, banners, icons, and presentations directly from your descriptions using AI.
actual_capabilities: Generate logos in 55+ styles with AI using Gemini for brand identity projects
Create complete corporate identity programs with 50+ mockup deliverables per brand
Design SVG icons in 15 different visual styles with Gemini 3.1 Pro
Build HTML presentation slides with Chart.js data visualizations and copywriting formulas
Create social media images optimized for Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube, Pinterest, TikTok, and Threads
Design banners in 22 styles for social media, ads, web, and print use cases
limitations: Requires a valid Gemini API key set as GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY environment variable
Does not produce vector AI or editable PSD source files, only SVG, HTML, and PNG output
Output quality depends on Gemini model capabilities and prompt specificity from the user
Cannot edit existing designs or modify uploaded images, only generate new content

use_cases.title: Startup Brand Launch
use_cases.description: Create a full brand identity including logo, color palette, brand guidelines, and mockup deliverables for a new business launch.

use_cases.title: Social Media Campaign Graphics
use_cases.description: Generate platform-optimized social media images and banners for marketing campaigns across multiple social networks.

use_cases.title: Data-Driven Presentation Slides
use_cases.description: Build professional HTML slide decks with Chart.js data visualizations, custom layouts, and copywriting formulas for business pitches.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Logo Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a logo for my {business_type} called {company_name}. Use {color_preference} colors and a {style} design style. Provide the result as SVG with color palette information.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting a custom logo for a new business

prompt_templates.title: Full Brand Identity Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete corporate identity program for {company_name} in the {industry} industry. Include logo variations, color palette, typography guidelines, business card design, letterhead, and social media profile templates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a complete brand identity system

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform Social Graphics
prompt_templates.prompt: Design social media images for {campaign_name}. Create versions for Instagram (1080x1080), LinkedIn (1200x627), Facebook (1200x630), Twitter (1200x675), and YouTube banner (2560x1440) with consistent branding.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a coordinated social media campaign

prompt_templates.title: Data Presentation with Charts
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an HTML slide deck about {topic}. Include a title slide with {title_text}, {N} content slides with Chart.js bar and line chart visualizations showing {data_description}, and a summary closing slide with key takeaways.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing a business presentation with data

output_examples.input: Design a minimalist logo for a coffee shop called Bean and Brew with warm brown colors.
output_examples.output: Created a logo package with three variations: a horizontal wordmark logo, a vertical stacked version, and a circular icon-only badge. The color palette uses warm brown (#6B3A2A) and cream (#D4A574) with a clean sans-serif font pairing.

output_examples.input: Create social media images for a summer sale campaign across Instagram and Facebook.
output: Generated 2 Instagram square posts (1080x1080px) with summer beach background gradients, sale text overlays, and a call-to-action button
Created 2 Facebook feed images (1200x630px) with matching branding, product highlights, and promotional discount banners

output_examples.input: Build a 5-slide HTML presentation about Q2 sales growth for a tech startup.
output_examples.output: Produced an interactive HTML slide deck with a gradient title slide, agenda overview, two Chart.js bar chart slides showing quarterly growth, and a closing slide with bullet-point key metrics and next steps.

best_practices: Provide specific style preferences such as colors, industry, mood, and design references for better generation results.
Use clear, descriptive prompts with exact dimensions and format requirements when requesting social images or banners.
Review AI-generated output and provide feedback or refinement requests to iterate toward the desired result.
anti_patterns: Do not request designs without providing any style context, color preferences, or industry information.
Do not use the skill for creating copyrighted or trademarked material without proper authorization and rights.
Do not expect pixel-perfect output on the first attempt without providing reference examples or detailed specifications.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Gemini API key. Set it as the GEMINI_API_KEY or GOOGLE_API_KEY environment variable before using the skill.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill generates SVG icons and logos, HTML slides with Chart.js, PNG image screenshots, and mockup images in standard web formats.

faq.question: Can I use generated designs for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, generated designs can be used for commercial projects. Review the Gemini API terms of service for specific usage rights and limitations.

faq.question: How many logo styles are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports over 55 logo styles across different industries and design approaches, including minimalist, vintage, modern, and geometric styles.

faq.question: Does this replace professional design software?
faq.answer: No. This is an AI-assisted design tool best for rapid prototyping, ideation, and creating assets for small to medium projects. Professional software is needed for advanced editing.

faq.question: Can I edit the designs after generation?
faq.answer: SVG and HTML outputs can be edited with standard tools. PNG images may need raster editing software for modifications after generation.
